exercises.title: Identify cascading failure scenario
exercises.instructions:
Razorpay payment service: 200 threads total, no bulkhead.
HDFC gateway responds in 8s (normal: 200ms).

Timeline: each payment request takes a thread for the gateway call duration.
At 08:00: 25 simultaneous HDFC payment requests arrive.
At 08:01: 50 more requests arrive (mix of HDFC, VISA, KYC).
At 08:02: 100 more requests arrive.

1. At 08:01: how many threads available for VISA requests?
2. At 08:02: how many threads available for any request?
3. What error do VISA users see even though VISA gateway is healthy?
4. With bulkhead (HDFC=40, VISA=80, KYC=40): how many HDFC threads before blocking?
exercises.expected_solution:
1. At 08:01 available threads for VISA:
   08:00: 25 HDFC requests → 25 threads stuck for 8s each
   08:01: 50 more arrive → 50 threads consumed
   Total consumed: 25 + 50 = 75 threads (HDFC + mix all sharing same pool)
   Remaining for VISA: 200 - 75 = 125 threads
   BUT if all 50 new requests include 50 HDFC: only 200-75=125 left
   If 30 HDFC + 20 VISA: VISA has 125 threads but their turn comes fine

2. At 08:02 threads for any request:
   Previous 75 still stuck (8s each, only 1s has passed)
   100 more arrive → if all grab threads: 75 + 100 = 175 threads consumed
   Remaining: 200 - 175 = 25 threads (near full)
   One more HDFC wave: thread pool exhausted → ALL requests fail

3. VISA users see despite VISA being healthy:
   503 Service Unavailable or connection timeout
   Thread pool is exhausted: no threads to handle the VISA request
   Spring/Tomcat: 'No threads available' → drops the request
   Cascading failure: HDFC problem causes VISA failure

4. With bulkhead (HDFC=40, VISA=80, KYC=40):
   HDFC bulkhead: after 40 concurrent HDFC calls → BulkheadFullException
   41st HDFC request: immediate 429 (not waiting 8s)
   VISA bulkhead: still has all 80 threads → VISA working normally
   KYC bulkhead: still has all 40 threads → KYC working normally
   Total threads used: 40+80+40=160 (doesn't have to match server thread pool)

test_cases.expected:
1. At 08:01 available threads for VISA:
   08:00: 25 HDFC requests → 25 threads stuck for 8s each
   08:01: 50 more arrive → 50 threads consumed
   Total consumed: 25 + 50 = 75 threads (HDFC + mix all sharing same pool)
   Remaining for VISA: 200 - 75 = 125 threads
   BUT if all 50 new requests include 50 HDFC: only 200-75=125 left
   If 30 HDFC + 20 VISA: VISA has 125 threads but their turn comes fine

2. At 08:02 threads for any request:
   Previous 75 still stuck (8s each, only 1s has passed)
   100 more arrive → if all grab threads: 75 + 100 = 175 threads consumed
   Remaining: 200 - 175 = 25 threads (near full)
   One more HDFC wave: thread pool exhausted → ALL requests fail

3. VISA users see despite VISA being healthy:
   503 Service Unavailable or connection timeout
   Thread pool is exhausted: no threads to handle the VISA request
   Spring/Tomcat: 'No threads available' → drops the request
   Cascading failure: HDFC problem causes VISA failure

4. With bulkhead (HDFC=40, VISA=80, KYC=40):
   HDFC bulkhead: after 40 concurrent HDFC calls → BulkheadFullException
   41st HDFC request: immediate 429 (not waiting 8s)
   VISA bulkhead: still has all 80 threads → VISA working normally
   KYC bulkhead: still has all 40 threads → KYC working normally
   Total threads used: 40+80+40=160 (doesn't have to match server thread pool)

hints: Thread pool exhaustion: all threads waiting → new requests rejected
Bulkhead caps: 41st HDFC → BulkheadFull (immediate) vs 8s timeout
Cascading: healthy VISA fails because HDFC consumed threads

exercises.title: Complete the Resilience4j @Bulkhead annotation
exercises.instructions: Fill in the Bulkhead annotation and fallback method.

blanks.hint: fallback method name (match the fallbackMethod= value)
error code identifying which service is unavailable

hints: fallbackMethod: string name of the fallback method in same class
BulkheadFullException: last parameter of fallback method
Same return type: fallback must return same type as the main method

exercises.title: Bulkhead configuration for payment gateways
exercises.instructions:
Write Resilience4j BulkheadConfig for 3 gateways:
1. hdfcGateway: max 40 concurrent, 100ms wait
2. visaNetwork: max 40 concurrent, 100ms wait
3. aadharKyc: max 20 concurrent, 50ms wait
4. @SpringBootTest: BulkheadRegistry contains all 3
hints: BulkheadConfig.custom(): builder for custom config
maxConcurrentCalls: semaphore limit for concurrent calls
maxWaitDuration: how long to wait for a permit before throwing BulkheadFullException

exercises.title: Semaphore bulkhead with manual acquire/release
exercises.instructions:
Demonstrate manual Bulkhead usage (programmatic, no annotation):
1. Bulkhead.acquirePermission(): try to get a slot
2. If acquired: call downstream, then Bulkhead.onComplete()
3. If not acquired: return fallback immediately
4. @SpringBootTest: acquire + complete cycles correctly
hints: tryAcquirePermission(): non-blocking acquire (returns false if full)
onComplete() in finally: ALWAYS release even if exception thrown
getAvailableConcurrentCalls(): shows remaining permits

exercises.title: @Bulkhead with @CircuitBreaker combination
exercises.instructions:
Combine Bulkhead + CircuitBreaker for HDFC gateway:
1. @Bulkhead: prevent thread starvation (max 40 concurrent)
2. @CircuitBreaker: stop calls when HDFC fails >50%
3. Single method with both annotations
4. Fallback: returns fallback response for BOTH exceptions
5. @SpringBootTest: service instantiates with both beans
hints: @CircuitBreaker outer, @Bulkhead inner: CB checks state first, then BH checks concurrency
Multiple fallback overloads: Spring picks most specific exception type
BulkheadFullException vs CallNotPermittedException: different failure modes, different messages

exercises.title: Kubernetes sidecar pod spec
exercises.instructions:
Write Kubernetes Pod spec with sidecars:
1. Main container: payment-service (port 8080)
2. Sidecar 1: fluent-bit log aggregator
3. Sidecar 2: envoy proxy
4. Shared volume: /var/log
5. Explain: how sidecars share network namespace
exercises.expected_solution:
# Kubernetes Pod spec with sidecars:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: payment-service-pod
  labels:
    app: payment-service
    version: v2.1.0
spec:
  containers:
  # ---- MAIN CONTAINER ----
  - name: payment-service
    image: razorpay/payment-service:2.1.0
    ports:
    - containerPort: 8080  # HTTP API
    env:
    - name: LOG_PATH
      value: /var/log/payment-service
    volumeMounts:
    - name: log-volume
      mountPath: /var/log
    resources:
      limits:
        cpu: '1'
        memory: '512Mi'

  # ---- SIDECAR 1: Log Aggregation ----
  - name: fluent-bit
    image: fluent/fluent-bit:2.2.0
    volumeMounts:
    - name: log-volume
      mountPath: /var/log          # SAME volume: reads payment-service logs
    - name: fluent-bit-config
      mountPath: /fluent-bit/etc
    # Reads /var/log/*.log, ships to Elasticsearch
    # No code changes needed in payment-service!

  # ---- SIDECAR 2: Envoy Proxy (Service Mesh) ----
  - name: envoy-proxy
    image: envoyproxy/envoy:v1.28.0
    ports:
    - containerPort: 15001        # Intercepts all outbound traffic
    - containerPort: 15006        # Intercepts all inbound traffic
    # Provides: mTLS, circuit breaking, retries, distributed tracing
    # payment-service calls http://fraud-service (no TLS in app code)
    # Envoy: adds mTLS automatically (app doesn't know)

  volumes:
  - name: log-volume
    emptyDir: {}                  # Shared ephemeral storage
  - name: fluent-bit-config
    configMap:
      name: fluent-bit-config

# KEY SIDECAR BENEFITS:
# 1. Shared network namespace: all containers on localhost (127.0.0.1)
#    payment-service connects to envoy on localhost:15001
#    envoy connects to fraud-service (external)
# 2. Shared volumes: fluent-bit reads payment-service log files
# 3. Same lifecycle: containers start/stop together with the pod
# 4. Cross-cutting concerns: logging, mTLS, tracing — without app code changes

public class SidecarDoc { public static void main(String[] a){
    System.out.println("Sidecar: same pod, shared localhost network and volumes");
} }

expected_stdout_contains: Sidecar: same pod, shared localhost network and volumes

hints: emptyDir volume: ephemeral shared storage within pod lifetime
Shared network namespace: all containers reach each other on localhost
Envoy sidecar: intercepts traffic at pod level (app doesn't need TLS code)

exercises.title: Bulkhead metrics with Micrometer
exercises.instructions:
Add Micrometer metrics to Bulkhead:
1. bulkhead.available_concurrent_calls gauge (current permits)
2. bulkhead.rejected_calls counter (BulkheadFullException count)
3. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): gauge reads available permits, rejected increments on full
hints: Gauge: reads current available permits at scrape time
rejected counter: alert when high — indicates bulkhead too small or downstream too slow
tryAcquirePermission without onComplete: permits stay consumed (needed for second test)

exercises.title: Thread pool bulkhead (heavier isolation)
exercises.instructions:
Configure Resilience4j ThreadPoolBulkhead for HDFC:
1. Core threads: 20, max: 40, queue: 10
2. ThreadPoolBulkheadService.callHdfc(): executes in HDFC's thread pool
3. Returns CompletableFuture<String>
4. @SpringBootTest: call executes, returns result
hints: executeSupplier: run Supplier in the thread pool bulkhead
CompletableFuture: returned immediately; actual work runs in HDFC's pool
coreThreadPoolSize=5 for test: reduced from production 20 (faster startup)

exercises.title: Vault agent sidecar secret injection
exercises.instructions:
Design Vault Agent sidecar that injects secrets as files:
1. Vault agent reads secrets, writes to /vault/secrets/config.env
2. Spring Boot reads from that file (not from Vault SDK)
3. @Value with file-based property source
4. @SpringBootTest: properties from 'file' injected correctly
hints: spring.config.import=file:/vault/secrets/: Spring Boot reads properties from Vault agent output
No Vault SDK: app just reads @Value — simpler, language agnostic
Vault agent re-injects on lease renewal: Spring Boot picks up changes at next restart

exercises.title: Bulkhead sizing analysis
exercises.instructions:
Razorpay payment service:
- 200 Tomcat threads total
- 4 downstream gateways: HDFC, VISA, KYC, FraudDetection
- Traffic ratio: HDFC 40%, VISA 30%, KYC 20%, Fraud 10%
- Average response times: HDFC 200ms, VISA 150ms, KYC 300ms, Fraud 100ms
- Peak load: 500 req/s

For each gateway, calculate:
1. Expected concurrent calls at peak
2. Recommended bulkhead size (add 20% buffer)
3. If HDFC goes down (10s timeout), how many threads saved with bulkhead?
exercises.expected_solution:
1. Expected concurrent calls at peak:
   HDFC: 500 × 0.40 = 200 req/s × 0.200s = 40 concurrent calls
   VISA: 500 × 0.30 = 150 req/s × 0.150s = 22.5 ≈ 23 concurrent calls
   KYC:  500 × 0.20 = 100 req/s × 0.300s = 30 concurrent calls
   Fraud:500 × 0.10 = 50 req/s  × 0.100s = 5 concurrent calls
   Total: 40+23+30+5 = 98 concurrent (well within 200 Tomcat threads)

2. Recommended bulkhead size (+20% buffer):
   HDFC: 40 × 1.20 = 48 → round to 50
   VISA: 23 × 1.20 = 28 → round to 30
   KYC:  30 × 1.20 = 36 → round to 40
   Fraud: 5 × 1.20 = 6 → round to 10 (minimum)
   Total: 50+30+40+10 = 130 (leaves 70 Tomcat threads as general buffer)

3. Threads saved when HDFC goes down (10s timeout):
   Without bulkhead: 200 req/s HDFC × 10s = 2000 threads needed!
   But only 200 threads total → exhausted almost immediately
   VISA/KYC/Fraud: 0 threads → all 503

   With bulkhead (HDFC limit=50):
   HDFC requests: after 50 threads stuck → BulkheadFullException
   Additional HDFC requests: immediate 429 (no new thread consumed)
   VISA threads: still 30 available → VISA working normally
   KYC threads: still 40 available → KYC working normally
   Threads saved: 200 - 50 = 150 threads protected from HDFC timeout!
   Flipkart VISA payments: still processing at normal speed

test_cases.expected:
1. Expected concurrent calls at peak:
   HDFC: 500 × 0.40 = 200 req/s × 0.200s = 40 concurrent calls
   VISA: 500 × 0.30 = 150 req/s × 0.150s = 22.5 ≈ 23 concurrent calls
   KYC:  500 × 0.20 = 100 req/s × 0.300s = 30 concurrent calls
   Fraud:500 × 0.10 = 50 req/s  × 0.100s = 5 concurrent calls
   Total: 40+23+30+5 = 98 concurrent (well within 200 Tomcat threads)

2. Recommended bulkhead size (+20% buffer):
   HDFC: 40 × 1.20 = 48 → round to 50
   VISA: 23 × 1.20 = 28 → round to 30
   KYC:  30 × 1.20 = 36 → round to 40
   Fraud: 5 × 1.20 = 6 → round to 10 (minimum)
   Total: 50+30+40+10 = 130 (leaves 70 Tomcat threads as general buffer)

3. Threads saved when HDFC goes down (10s timeout):
   Without bulkhead: 200 req/s HDFC × 10s = 2000 threads needed!
   But only 200 threads total → exhausted almost immediately
   VISA/KYC/Fraud: 0 threads → all 503

   With bulkhead (HDFC limit=50):
   HDFC requests: after 50 threads stuck → BulkheadFullException
   Additional HDFC requests: immediate 429 (no new thread consumed)
   VISA threads: still 30 available → VISA working normally
   KYC threads: still 40 available → KYC working normally
   Threads saved: 200 - 50 = 150 threads protected from HDFC timeout!
   Flipkart VISA payments: still processing at normal speed

hints: Little's Law: concurrent calls = throughput × latency
Buffer: +20% for burst traffic
Threads saved: total - bulkhead limit = protected from cascading failure

exercises.title: Complete Razorpay bulkhead isolation
exercises.instructions:
Build complete bulkhead isolation:
1. PaymentGatewayService with 3 @Bulkhead methods (hdfc, visa, kyc)
2. Fallback for each: {status: FALLBACK, gateway: NAME}
3. Micrometer: rejected_calls counter per gateway
4. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): normal call, bulkhead full→fallback, metric increments
hints: Manual bulkhead (no annotation): gives test control over permit counts
tryAcquirePermission without onComplete: permits stay consumed (for test setup)
bulkhead.rejected counter: alert threshold — too many rejections = downstream unhealthy

exercises.title: Envoy sidecar — service mesh configuration
exercises.instructions:
Document Envoy proxy sidecar for Razorpay payment service:
1. Envoy config: upstream clusters for fraud-service + notification-service
2. Circuit breaker in Envoy (not Spring code)
3. mTLS between payment-service and fraud-service
4. @SpringBootTest: demonstrate service-to-service call via localhost proxy
exercises.starter_code: // Envoy sidecar configuration documentation + Spring code
hints: Envoy intercepts on localhost: app calls http://, Envoy adds TLS transparently
Envoy circuit breaker: at TCP level across all languages/frameworks
mTLS via sidecar: certificate management outside app code

exercises.title: Complete Razorpay gateway bulkhead suite
exercises.instructions:
Build complete gateway bulkhead suite:
1. 3 gateways: HDFC, VISA, KYC with different bulkhead sizes
2. GatewayRouter: selects gateway based on merchant preference, falls back to next
3. Bulkhead exhausted → try next gateway, then fallback
4. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): HDFC works, HDFC full→VISA, both full→KYC, all full→fallback
hints: exhaustBulkhead: drain all permits without releasing (simulates full pool)
While loop: tryAcquirePermission until false (all permits consumed)
Gateway cascade: HDFC → VISA → KYC → ultimate fallback

exercises.title: Fluent Bit sidecar log aggregation
exercises.instructions:
Design Fluent Bit sidecar log aggregation for payment service:
1. PaymentService writes structured logs to /var/log/payment.log
2. Fluent Bit config: reads logs, adds pod metadata, ships to Elasticsearch
3. Log format: JSON with traceId, merchantId, amount
4. @SpringBootTest: structured log output is JSON-parseable
hints: Structured JSON logs: Fluent Bit parses automatically (no regex needed)
log.info(json): writes to standard output → captured by Fluent Bit
No Elasticsearch SDK: app just logs, sidecar handles shipping

exercises.title: Complete M24 T5 Bulkhead Sidecar synthesis
exercises.instructions:
Build complete M24 T5 synthesis:
1. Bulkhead for HDFC (size=3) with fallback
2. Bulkhead metrics: rejected counter + available gauge
3. StructuredLogger: JSON log output
4. GatewayHealthReport: reports bulkhead status
5. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): normal call, full→fallback, metric increments, log is JSON
6. All 4 pass
exercises.starter_code: // Complete M24 T5 Bulkhead Sidecar synthesis
hints: Gauge registration: in constructor (once, not per call)
exhaust(): while loop consuming all permits without releasing
rejected counter: assert >=1 (other tests in suite may also increment)
